title: Pleopeltis zollingeriana
taxonomy: Pleopeltis zollingeriana is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia. The classification of Pleopeltis zollingeriana places it within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Polypodiaceae. This species was previously classified under the genus Colysis, but it has since been reclassified under the genus Pleopeltis. Further research may be needed to confirm its exact classification within the family Polypodiaceae.
uses: Pleopeltis zollingeriana is a species of epiphytic fern, and research suggests that it has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. In some parts of the world, it is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antihypertensive properties, which may help in reducing blood pressure. However, more studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pleopeltis zollingeriana for medicinal purposes.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial in treating various health issues. intro: Pleopeltis zollingeriana, commonly known as the Zollinger's polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis zollingeriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
